You are a senior software engineer implementing one phase of a larger project.

        ## Project: [SupportAgent Workflow Builder]
        Design an agentic SOP execution system for automated customer support ticket routing, triage, and draft response generation.

## Full Project Master Plan
# SupportAgent Workflow Builder — Master Implementation Plan

## Idea Overview

An agentic SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) execution system for automated customer support ticket routing, triage, and draft response generation. The system ingests support tickets from multiple channels, classifies and prioritizes them, routes them to the correct resolution path, and produces draft responses — all driven by configurable, versioned workflows.

---

## Architecture Notes

### High-Level Architecture

```
┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────┐     ┌──────────────────┐
│  Ticket Ingest│────▶│  Triage &    │────▶│  Routing Engine  │
│  (Email, Chat,│     │  Classify    │     │  (SOP Router)    │
│   Web, API)   │     │              │     │                  │
└──────────────┘     └──────────────┘     └────────┬─────────┘
                                                    │
                                    ┌───────────────┼───────────────┐
                                    ▼               ▼               ▼
                             ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────┐
                             │ Auto-    │   │ Escalate │   │ Assign   │
                             │ Resolve  │   │ to Human │   │ to Agent │
                             └──────────┘   └──────────┘   └──────────┘
                                    │
                                    ▼
                             ┌──────────────┐
                             │ Draft Response│
                             │ Generator     │
                             └──────────────┘
                                    │
                                    ▼
                             ┌──────────────┐
                             │  Output &     │
                             │  Notifications│
                             └──────────────┘
```

### Key Components

| Component | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| **Ticket Ingest Layer** | Normalizes incoming tickets from email, web forms, chat, and API into a unified `Ticket` model |
| **Triage Engine** | Classifies ticket category, urgency, and sentiment; applies priority scoring |
| **SOP Router** | Matches tickets to the correct Standard Operating Procedure; selects resolution path |
| **Workflow Engine** | Executes multi-step SOPs with conditional branching, human-in-the-loop gates, and escalation rules |
| **Response Generator** | Produces draft responses using template engine + LLM fallback; supports tone/style customization |
| **Agent Orchestrator** | Coordinates multi-agent workflows; manages handoffs between auto-resolve, human review, and escalation paths |
| **Integration Layer** | Connects to external systems (CRM, ticketing, email, chat platforms) |
| **Analytics & Feedback** | Tracks SLA compliance, resolution quality, agent performance; feeds feedback into workflow improvement |

### Technology Decisions

- **Workflow DSL**: YAML-based SOP definitions with versioning (enables non-engineer configuration)
- **Routing**: Rule-base

        ## Phase 1 of 3
        ## Phase Spec
## Phase 1 — Core Ticket Pipeline (MVP)

> **Goal:** A working system that ingests a ticket, classifies it, routes it, and produces a draft response — end to end.

### Description

Build the foundational ticket processing pipeline. This includes a unified ticket model, a rule-based classifier, a simple SOP router, and a template-driven response generator. The system processes tickets through a linear pipeline: ingest → classify → route → draft → output. No multi-step workflows or agent orchestration yet — just a single, reliable path that handles the most common support scenarios.

### Deliverable

A runnable service (CLI + REST API) that:
- Accepts a ticket via API or file input
- Classifies it into one of ~10 predefined categories (billing, technical, account, etc.)
- Routes it to the correct team based on category + keyword rules
- Generates a draft response using a template engine with variable substitution
- Outputs the draft response as JSON + sends a notification (console log or email stub)

### Dependencies

- None (foundation phase — builds nothing on top of other project phases)

### Success Criteria

- [ ] Ticket ingestion accepts ≥3 input formats (JSON, email MIME, web form payload)
- [ ] Classification accuracy ≥ 85% on a test set of 500 labeled tickets
- [ ] Routing correctly assigns tickets to teams in ≥ 90% of test cases
- [ ] Draft response generation completes in < 2 seconds per ticket
- [ ] End-to-end pipeline latency p95 < 3 seconds
- [ ] Template system supports ≥ 5 distinct response templates
- [ ] API returns structured output with ticket ID, category, team, and draft body
- [ ] Unit test coverage ≥ 70% on core pipeline logic

---

        ## Tasks & Acceptance Criteria
        ## Phase 1 — SupportAgent Workflow Builder (Tasks 1-5)
- [x] Task 1: Core data models (Ticket, PipelineResult, enums)
- [x] Task 2: Ingestor (JSON, MIME, web form parsing)
- [x] Task 3: Classifier (keyword-based categorization, urgency, priority scoring)
- [x] Task 4: Router (team assignment, escalation rules, SLA checks)
- [x] Task 5: Review (code review, bug fix, test verification) — All 4 blocking bugs fixed, 35/35 tests passing

# SupportAgent Workflow Builder — Master Implementation Plan

## Idea Overview

An agentic SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) execution system for automated customer support ticket routing, triage, and draft response generation. The system ingests support tickets from multiple channels, classifies and prioritizes them, routes them to the correct resolution path, and produces draft responses — all driven by configurable, versioned workflows.

        ## Phase 2 of 3
        ## Phase Spec
## Phase 2 — SOP Engine & Enhanced Triage

> **Goal:** Add configurable multi-step workflows, advanced triage, and LLM-assisted response generation.

### Description

Extend the pipeline with a full SOP execution engine that supports multi-step workflows with conditional branching, human-in-the-loop gates, and escalation rules. Add an advanced triage engine with sentiment analysis, priority scoring, and ML-assisted classification. Introduce LLM-assisted draft response generation for complex or novel ticket types that don't match existing templates.

### Deliverable

- **SOP DSL & Engine**: YAML-based workflow definitions with support for:
  - Multi-step sequences with conditional branches
  - Human-in-the-loop approval gates
  - Escalation rules (timeout-based, confidence-based, category-based)
  - Workflow versioning and rollback
- **Enhanced Triage**:
  - Sentiment analysis (positive / neutral / negative / angry)
  - Priority scoring (1–5 scale) based on urgency signals
  - ML classifier for auto-categorization (fine-tuned or zero-shot)
- **LLM Response Generator**:
  - Template fallback for known patterns
  - LLM generation for unknown patterns with confidence scoring
  - Tone/style customization per team or customer tier
- **Workflow Builder UI (minimal)**:
  - Visual SOP editor (drag-and-drop or form-based)
  - Import/export SOPs
  - Version history

### Dependencies

- Phase 1 (core ticket pipeline must be stable and tested)

### Success Criteria

- [ ] SOP engine executes ≥ 5 distinct multi-step workflows correctly
- [ ] Human-in-the-loop gates pause and resume workflows without data loss
- [ ] Escalation rules trigger correctly in ≥ 95% of test scenarios
- [ ] ML classifier accuracy ≥ 90% on held-out test set
- [ ] Sentiment analysis F1 ≥ 0.80
- [ ] LLM draft responses rated "acceptable" by ≥ 80% of human reviewers
- [ ] SOP versioning supports ≥ 3 versions per workflow with rollback
- [ ] Workflow Builder UI can create a new SOP in < 5 minutes
- [ ] Integration with at least 2 external systems (e.g., email + CRM)

---



        ## Tasks & Acceptance Criteria
        # Phase 2 Tasks

- [ ] Task 1: SOP DSL Engine — YAML workflow definitions, multi-step execution, conditional branching, and human-in-the-loop gates
  - What: Build the core SOP engine that reads YAML workflow definitions and executes multi-step sequences with conditional branches and human-in-the-loop approval gates. Each step in a workflow can be an action (e.g., "assign_team", "send_email", "generate_draft"), a condition (e.g., "if priority >= 8"), or a gate (e.g., "await human approval"). The engine maintains execution state so workflows can be paused at gates and resumed later without data loss.
  - Files: 
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/sop_engine.py` — `SOPWorkflow` model, `SOPStep`, `SOPCondition`, `SOPGate`, `WorkflowEngine` classes
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/sop_engine.py` — `WorkflowState` dataclass for pause/resume state
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/config/sop_workflows/` directory with 5 sample YAML workflow files (billing_flow.yaml, technical_flow.yaml, account_flow.yaml, escalation_flow.yaml, general_flow.yaml)
    - Modify `workspace/supportagent/models.py` — add `WorkflowState` and `SOPStepResult` to the model exports
  - Done when: 
    - Engine can load and execute 5 distinct multi-step YAML workflows end-to-end
    - Conditional branches correctly route execution based on ticket attributes (priority, category, sentiment)
    - Human-in-the-loop gates pause execution, persist state to a `WorkflowState` object, and resume without data loss
    - Unit tests cover: workflow loading, step execution, condition evaluation, gate pause/resume, escalation trigger, and error handling (≥ 20 tests)

- [ ] Task 2: Workflow Versioning & Rollback System
  - What: Add version management for SOP workflows. Each workflow definition is stored with a version number. The system supports creating new versions, listing versions, and rolling back to any prior version. Version metadata includes timestamp, author, and change summary. Rollback atomically replaces the active workflow with a previous version.
  - Files:
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/sop_versioning.py` — `WorkflowVersion`, `VersionStore`, `VersionManager` classes
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/config/sop_versions/` directory for versioned workflow storage
    - Modify `workspace/supportagent/sop_engine.py` — integrate version lookup so the engine loads the active version by default
    - Add CLI commands in `workspace/supportagent/cli.py` for version management (list, rollback, diff)
  - Done when:
    - Each workflow supports ≥ 3 versions with full metadata (timestamp, author, change summary)
    - Rollback atomically switches the active workflow version and the engine picks up the new version on next load
    - Version diff shows step-by-step changes between versions
    - Unit tests cover: version creation, listing, rollback, diff generation, and concurrent version safety (≥ 10 tests)

- [ ] Task 3: Enhanced Triage Engine — Sentiment analysis, priority scoring, and ML-assisted classification
  - What: Extend the existing `Classifier` with three capabilities: (1) Sentiment analysis that classifies ticket text as positive / neutral / negative / angry using a lexicon-based approach (no external ML dependency required for MVP, but architecture supports swapping in an ML model later). (2) Priority scoring on a 1–5 scale based on urgency signals (keywords, sentiment, customer tier, SLA status). (3) ML-assisted auto-categorization using a zero-shot classification approach (e.g., using sentence embeddings + cosine similarity to category prototypes, or a lightweight transformer if available).
  - Files:
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/enhanced_triage.py` — `SentimentAnalyzer`, `PriorityScorer`, `MLClassifier` classes
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/config/sentiment_lexicon.yaml` — word lists for positive, neutral, negative, angry sentiment
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/config/ml_category_prototypes.yaml` — category prototype vectors for zero-shot classification
    - Modify `workspace/supportagent/classifier.py` — integrate enhanced triage as a pipeline step; add `EnhancedClassifier` wrapper that calls sentiment, priority, and ML classifier in sequence
    - Modify `workspace/supportagent/models.py` — add `Sentiment` enum and update `Ticket` to include enhanced triage fields
  - Done when:
    - Sentiment analyzer correctly classifies ≥ 80% of test tickets (F1 ≥ 0.80 on held-out test set of 100 labeled tickets)
    - Priority scorer produces 1–5 scores that correlate with urgency signals (verified via test assertions)
    - ML classifier achieves ≥ 90% accuracy on a held-out test set of 200 labeled tickets
    - Unit tests cover: sentiment analysis (all 4 classes), priority scoring edge cases, ML classification accuracy, and integration with existing Classifier (≥ 25 tests)

- [ ] Task 4: LLM-Assisted Response Generator — Template fallback, LLM generation, and tone/style customization
  - What: Build a response generator that first attempts template-based matching for known ticket patterns (using Jinja2 templates from `supportagent/config/templates/`). For unknown or novel ticket types, it falls back to LLM-assisted generation with confidence scoring. Supports tone/style customization per team or customer tier (e.g., formal for enterprise, casual for startup). The generator produces draft responses with metadata including confidence score, generation method (template vs. LLM), and applicable tone.
  - Files:
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/response_generator.py` — `TemplateResponseGenerator`, `LLMResponseGenerator`, `ResponseGenerator` (orchestrator) classes
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/config/response_templates/` directory with ≥ 5 Jinja2 templates (billing, technical, account, urgent, general)
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/config/tone_styles.yaml` — tone/style definitions per team and customer tier
    - Modify `workspace/supportagent/models.py` — add `ResponseMetadata` dataclass with confidence_score, generation_method, tone fields
    - Modify `workspace/supportagent/router.py` — integrate response generation into the routing pipeline
  - Done when:
    - Template matching correctly generates responses for all 5 template types with variable substitution
    - LLM fallback generates acceptable responses for unmatched ticket types with confidence scoring
    - Tone customization works per team and customer tier (verified via test assertions on output text)
    - Unit tests cover: template rendering, LLM fallback simulation, tone customization, confidence scoring, and pipeline integration (≥ 20 tests)

- [ ] Task 5: Workflow Builder UI (minimal) and External System Integration Layer
  - What: Build a minimal web-based Workflow Builder UI using FastAPI (already in dependencies) with a simple HTML/JS frontend for visual SOP editing. The UI supports form-based SOP creation (step-by-step form), import/export of YAML workflows, and version history display. Also build the integration layer that connects the system to at least 2 external systems (email via SMTP stub, CRM via REST API stub).
  - Files:
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/api.py` — FastAPI routes for workflow CRUD, version management, and ticket processing
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/ui/templates/` — HTML templates for the Workflow Builder UI (SOP editor, version history, import/export)
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/ui/static/` — minimal CSS/JS for the form-based SOP editor
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/integrations/` — `__init__.py`, `email_integration.py` (SMTP stub), `crm_integration.py` (REST API stub), `notification_service.py`
    - Create `workspace/supportagent/cli.py` — CLI entry point with commands for API server, workflow management, and integration testing
    - Modify `workspace/supportagent/__init__.py` — export new public classes
  - Done when:
    - UI can create a new SOP workflow via form-based editor in < 5 minutes
    - Impo
